issue_body:
Build the first screen creators see after opening the product: a simple library of shows and past episodes where they can start a new episode from an existing show template, review episode status, and keep multiple podcast identities separated. This makes reusable templates meaningful for repeat production and supports teams or agencies managing more than one show.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A user can create or select a show, see its saved template/style identity, view a list of episodes with clear statuses, and start a new episode prefilled with that show’s template and speaker/style defaults. The workflow is covered by automated tests, and `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The product already has the single-episode creation, editing, template, review, and export flow. The next missing product foundation is repeatability across shows and episodes, which is central to podcast teams and agencies and directly builds on the reusable template work without jumping into marketplace or advanced collaboration too early._
